methods.description: The interval approach compares derived enamel sample results with 87Sr/86Sr and δ18O isoscapes for the Caribbean to determine potential geographic origins by identifying raster cells with overlapping isotope ranges.

steps.description: Estimate total error/variance (Ɛ) including analytical error, population variance, and mean absolute error (MAE) of interpolated isoscapes.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate total error/variance for isotope values

steps.description: Use measured enamel isotope data to conduct geographic assignments to maps of predicted enamel isotope composition.
steps.searchable_summary: assign geographic origins based on enamel isotope data

steps.description: Visualize results by coding raster cells as potential or non-potential places of origin based on overlap with measured isotope values.
steps.searchable_summary: visualize geographic origin assignments on map

methods.description: The likelihood approach uses dual-isotope values to assess geographic origin likelihood by calculating a spatially explicit normal probability density function, improving upon the interval approach by avoiding thresholding.

steps.description: Calculate likelihood of specific geographic origin using a spatially explicit normal probability density function.
steps.searchable_summary: compute likelihood of origin using normal pdf
outputs: likelihood values for each raster cell

steps.description: Evaluate likelihood at each cell to determine the most likely origin point.
steps.searchable_summary: evaluate likelihood to find most likely origin

steps.description: Visualize results using univariate and bivariate normal probability density functions to highlight areas of high likelihood of origin.
steps.searchable_summary: visualize high likelihood areas on map
outputs: visualized map with high likelihood areas
